summary: Recent updates to the testing area of the Firefox browser have introduced improvements and new tests focusing on media playback functionalities. Specifically, there are now fixes for seeking in HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) when certain frame types are present on macOS and resolved issues for Linux that were causing test failures. Additionally, **more robust unit tests** have been integrated, enhancing the reliability of the Media Foundation's usage in handling different media formats like MP4 and H.264. These changes help ensure smoother video playback experiences and prevent crashes, contributing to a more stable overall browser performance.
